story.iWant: to resolve path tokens independently of transfer operations
story.soThat: both Test-ArchiveExists and Send-Archive can use consistent destination paths
story.tasks: - Implement PathTokenResolver service (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4, #5) - Create PathTokenResolver class with ResolveTokens method - Implement token replacement logic for all supported tokens - Create TokenResolverContext record for input parameters - Handle all path types (UNC, S3, local) - Add error handling (AC: #6) - Create TokenResolutionException for invalid tokens - Validate template and context inputs - Create unit tests (AC: #6) - Test S3 path with {year}/{month}/{day} tokens - Test UNC path with {app}/{year} tokens - Test {server} token replacement - Test template with no tokens returns unchanged - Test missing required context throws exception

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Resolves tokens: {year}, {month}, {day}, {date}, {server}, {app} 2. Uses log date being archived, NOT current date 3. Accepts template string and date/context parameters 4. Returns fully resolved path string 5. Handles UNC paths (\\server\share), S3 paths (s3://bucket/key), local paths (D:\path) 6. Unit tests validate token replacement accuracy

docs.doc: PathTokenResolver: Internal Service to replace path tokens ({year}, {month}, etc.). Inputs: Template path, TokenResolverContext. Outputs: Resolved path string. Foundation Layer component.
TokenResolverContext record: { DateTime LogDate (date being processed, NOT current date), string ServerName ({server} token value), string ApplicationName ({app} token value) }

- Template "s3://bucket/{year}/{month}/{day}" + LogDate=2025-10-08 → "s3://bucket/2025/10/08" - Template "\\server\{app}\{year}-{month}" + AppName="MyApp", LogDate=2025-10-08 → "\\server\MyApp\2025-10" - Template with {server} token + ServerName="PROD-SVR01" → replaces {server} with "PROD-SVR01" - Template with no tokens → returns unchanged
TokenResolutionException extends LogManagerException. Used for invalid token resolution errors.

Foundation layer component with no dependencies. Critical for FR002 (Test-ArchiveExists) and FR006 (Send-Archive). Must be reusable utility function. Supported tokens: {year}, {month}, {day}, {date}, {server}, {app}.

artifact.reason: Example of C# record pattern used for Foundation layer models - follow same pattern for TokenResolverContext
Example of Foundation layer service pattern - PathTokenResolver should follow similar structure with public method and clear XML documentation
Current project configuration: .NET 9.0, C# 12, nullable enabled. PathTokenResolver must comply with these settings

constraints.constraint: Foundation layer - NO dependencies on other services or cmdlets
Must use C# record types for data models (modern .NET pattern per ADR-001)
Service class must be < 500 lines (ADR-004: Code maintainability)
Use log date being archived, NOT current date
Token format: case-sensitive, curly braces required
Must handle all path types: UNC (\\server\share), S3 (s3://bucket/key), local (D:\path)
Enable nullable reference types (Nullable=enable in csproj)
Provide complete XML documentation comments for public APIs

tests.standards: Unit testing framework: xUnit (version 2.6.6) following Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Use [Fact] for single test cases and [Theory] with [InlineData] for parameterized tests. Test coverage target: 100% for Foundation layer services per ADR-006. Mocking framework: Moq (version 4.20.70) for dependencies (though PathTokenResolver has none). Test execution: dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" Test naming convention: MethodName_Scenario_ExpectedResult
tests.locations: tests/logManager.Tests/Services/PathTokenResolverTests.cs (unit tests for PathTokenResolver) tests/logManager.Tests/Models/TokenResolverContextTests.cs (if validation logic exists) tests/logManager.Tests/Exceptions/TokenResolutionExceptionTests.cs (exception tests)

ideas.test: Test S3 path "s3://bucket/{year}/{month}/{day}" with LogDate=2025-10-08 returns "s3://bucket/2025/10/08"
Test UNC path "\\\\server\\{app}\\{year}" with AppName="MyApp", LogDate=2025-10-08 returns "\\\\server\\MyApp\\2025"
Test {server} token with ServerName="PROD-SVR01" replaced correctly
Test all tokens: {year}, {month}, {day}, {date}, {server}, {app} in single template
Test template with no tokens returns unchanged
Test missing required context (e.g., {server} token but ServerName null) throws TokenResolutionException
Verify uses LogDate from context, not DateTime.Today
Test local path with tokens: D:\\Logs\\{app}\\{date}
Test invalid token {invalid_token} throws TokenResolutionException or leaves unchanged (decide on behavior)
Test {month} token formats as zero-padded MM
Test {day} token formats as zero-padded DD
Test case sensitivity - {Year} should NOT match (only {year})
